extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won a controlled sprint in Châteauroux after KLUGE Roger and VAN AVERMAET Greg's two-man break was absorbed inside 2km
tactical_patterns: KLUGE Roger's Olympic track pedigree used in two-man break suggests targeting understrength chases before full sprint trains commit.
ASGREEN and VAN AVERMAET in early move with GC threat forced panic chase despite sprint teams represented showing coordination gaps.
DECEUNINCK quick-step controlled final kilometer with PHILIPPE Julian as world champion taking leadout duty revealing hierarchy depth.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: returning from near-retirement eight months prior, won with identical celebration to 2008 debut showing peak form restoration
rider_signals.KLUGE Roger: neutral: selected for two-man break quality but couldn't sustain gap against organized sprint team chase
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: only rider to bridge after initial break caught suggests solo strength but failed to gain meaningful advantage
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: world champion deployed as leadout rather than own sprint option shows team confidence in hierarchical sacrifice
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: neutral: took intermediate sprint points but unable to contest final sprint meaningfully despite positioning
rider_signals.GUARNIERI Jacopo: neutral: closest challenger to Cavendish but gap significant, crashed inside 2km without injury showing vulnerability under pressure
